channel.title: 简报 Jae Daily · 2026年04月02日
channel.description: 国际政治 · 宏观财经 · 前沿科技 · AI 辅助分析

item.title: [未译] AI benchmarks are broken. Here’s what we need instead.
item.description: For decades, artificial intelligence has been evaluated through the question of whether machines outperform humans. From chess to advanced math, from coding to essay writing, the performance of AI mod

item.title: [未译] Elon Musk's SpaceX moves to become a publicly traded company
item.description: The company’s public stock debut is set to be one of the most valuable in history, and could make Musk the world's first trillionaire.

item.title: [未译] Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
item.description: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

item.title: [未译] Pedro Sánchez, Lamine Yamal slam anti-Muslim chanting at Spain football game against Egypt
item.description: The prime minister rebuked an "uncivil minority" for targeting visiting fans and players, while a top Spain footballer called the incident "ignorant and racist."

item.title: [未译] Mass robotaxi malfunction halts traffic in Chinese city
item.description: Baidu has not responded to a request for comment about the outage, which affected at least 100 cars.

item.title: [未译] The Download: brainless human clones and the first uterus kept alive outside a body
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Inside the stealthy startup that pitched brainless

item.title: [未译] UK borrowers coming off five-year fixes face biggest mortgage ‘shock’
item.description: Those refinancing a five-year fix face paying an extra £395 a month on average — but will still be paying less than those who fixed two years ago

item.title: [未译] Japan Credit Pipeline Slows as Investors Grapple With Volatility
item.description: The pipeline for Japanese corporate bonds is the slowest since 2023 as investors contend with uncertainty about the Middle East war.

item.title: [未译] Australian State Triggers Emergency Powers Amid Fuel Crisis
item.description: Western Australia invoked emergency powers to force fuel suppliers to provide detailed information on their supply chains, as the nation seeks to manage an ongoing shortage spurred by panic-buying in

item.title: [未译] New laws to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: Consumers will be able to cancel unwanted subscriptions 'at the click of a button', the government said.

item.title: [未译] ASX’s Risk, Compliance Practices ‘Need to Mature,’ Probe Finds
item.description: ASX Ltd.’s risk and compliance failures could potentially have serious consequences that negatively impact Australia’s financial markets, a probe into the exchange operator found.

item.title: [未译] Labour must get out of its own way
item.description: The government’s approach to policymaking has been self-defeating

item.title: [未译] India’s RBI Tightens Rules Again for Offshore Rupee Trading
item.description: India’s monetary authority stepped up efforts to curb speculation against the battered rupee, restricting lenders from offering certain offshore foreign exchange derivative contracts, just days after

item.title: [未译] Shutting Hormuz is a template for China in Taiwan
item.description: Beijing will seek to replicate Tehran’s playbook in the Taiwan Strait — and the global economic impact could be even worse

item.title: [未译] OpenAI Is Falling Out of Favor With Secondary Buyers
item.description: OpenAI shares have fallen out of favor on the secondary market — in some cases becoming almost impossible to unload — as investors pivot quickly to Anthropic, its biggest competitor.

item.title: [未译] Minimum wage rises to £12.71 an hour as firms warn of impact
item.description: Many businesses have said they will have to pass higher wage costs onto customers.

item.title: [未译] Goodbye Graaff-Reinet: South African town’s name change stirs racial tensions
item.description: Minister’s decision to ditch town’s colonial-era identity and honour anti-apartheid activist divides residentsA South African town is divided over changing its name from the colonial-era Graaff-Reinet

item.title: [未译] Eli Lilly opposes push to pass Trump's drug pricing deals into law, CEO says
item.description: Eli Lilly opposes the White House's push to codify "most favored nation" drug pricing into law, CEO Dave Ricks said in an interview with CNBC.

item.title: [未译] Trump learns that not everyone has a price
item.description: The great cynic did not expect Iran to fight out of conviction

item.title: [未译] Starmer signals major UK pivot towards EU after Trump taunts
item.description: British prime minister says closer ties between London and Brussels are ‘in our long-term national interest’

item.title: [未译] Estate agents accuse Rightmove of charging excessive fees
item.description: The online listing portal is now being pursued in a class action, launched on behalf of potentially hundreds of estate agents.

item.title: [未译] Energy bill help would be based on household income, Reeves says
item.description: The chancellor tells the BBC it is "too early" to say exactly who would get help but hinted any support would not arrive until the autumn.

item.title: [未译] April Fools’ Day 2026: the best and cringiest pranks
item.description: Welcome to the worst day on the internet! As Chaim Gartenberg pointed out years ago, brands and a holiday dedicated to hoaxes are rarely a winning combo. If you’re a comp

item.title: [未译] Families cram into Greek court for trial into deadliest train crash
item.description: Fifty-seven people were killed when two trains collided in 2023 in the worst rail accident in Greek history, with 36 accused in a trial expected to last years.

item.title: [未译] Explosions at Burundi ammunition depot kill civilians, witnesses say
item.description: Powerful blasts destroyed homes and sparked panic in the city of Bujumbura on Tuesday night.

item.title: [未译] Peter Magyar, the former Orban ally vying for power in Hungary
item.description: Magyar represents the biggest threat to Viktor Orban's rule in Hungary since he won the first of four consecutive victories in 2010.

item.title: [未译] From water to council tax: How the bill rises (and one drop) affect you
item.description: A string of bill increases have taken effect but minimum wage and benefit rises will help some to pay them.

item.title: [未译] Devastated Italians reckon with 'third apocalypse' of World Cup failure
item.description: Azzurri fans and management alike are pondering what went wrong after Italy fail to qualify for third World Cup in a row.

item.title: [未译] Shifting to AI model customization is an architectural imperative
item.description: In the early days of large language models (LLMs), we grew accustomed to massive 10x jumps in reasoning and coding capability with every new model iteration. Today, those jumps have flattened into inc

item.title: [未译] FBI declares suspected Chinese hack of US surveillance system a ‘major cyber incident’
item.description: The designation suggests the hackers successfully compromised swathes of sensitive data stored directly on FBI systems.
